action.hypothesis: Round 52 was policy-rejected because it tried to retune `LR` on `gated@4`, which implicitly changed both `lr` and `k` relative to the family reference recipe. The corrected next single question is therefore to retune `LR` exactly on the current `gated` anchor itself: with `ARCHITECTURE=gated, K=128, EF=12, OPTIMIZER=signum` fixed and the now-proven `USE_HADAMARD=1` preprocessing kept on, does raising `LR` from `2.0e-3` to `2.4e-3` improve the full curve further, or is `2.0e-3` still the best step size even after Hadamard strengthened conditioning?
action.summary: Stay on the active `gated` mainline and return to the family reference point so the round passes single-variable policy cleanly. Keep `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1` fixed, and change only `LR` from `2.0e-3` to `2.4e-3`. Judge the run by the whole trajectory, not just the endpoint: if Hadamard has genuinely widened the stable step-size range, `2.4e-3` should preserve or improve early and mid descent and finish below the current `0.142923` anchor; if the curve gets flatter, noisier, or regresses, keep `2.0e-3` as the operative shared recipe and stop the LR branch again.
action.self_review: This fixes the Round 52 policy mistake by anchoring the LR question back on the current family reference recipe, so only one core axis changes. It also stays on the same family, respects `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, and asks a useful recipe question after the K sweep rather than reopening another family. The main risk is that `2.4e-3` already regressed before Hadamard, but the preprocess improvement materially changed the anchor, so one clean LR retest on the strengthened recipe is justified before treating the step size as fully settled.
notes_to_memory: Round 52 was policy-rejected because changing `LR` at `K=4` violated the param-only single-core-axis rule relative to the family reference recipe; future recipe retunes must be anchored back on the current reference point.
Round 53 cleanly reopens the LR question on the strengthened `gated@128` anchor itself by testing whether `USE_HADAMARD=1` makes `LR=2.4e-3` better than the current `2.0e-3` best.
next_hypotheses: If `LR=2.4e-3` improves the Hadamard-enabled `gated@128` curve or endpoint, run at most one nearby confirmation or adopt it as the new anchor before any further branch work.
If `LR=2.4e-3` is neutral or worse again, lock `2.0e-3` as the shared mainline LR and treat the remaining variation across K as not primarily a step-size issue.

Judge the run by the whole trajectory, not just the endpoint: if Hadamard has genuinely widened the stable step-size range, `2.4e-3` should preserve or improve early and mid descent and finish below the current `0.142923` anchor; if the curve gets flatter, noisier, or regresses, keep `2.0e-3` as the operative shared recipe and stop the LR branch again.
